reason: The first final review correctly rejected a detached teal eye layer in Down. After all eye translation was disabled and complete-head cues were rerendered, the duplicate eye disappeared, the face remained coherent, all validators passed, three fresh blind reviewers classified all four repaired directions correctly, and one native-size remediation reviewer plus the parent approved the restrained head motion. The second remediation reviewer considered the unlabeled native-size cue subtle; that dissent is preserved rather than forcing an exaggerated head rotation.
